cover: Split-screen comparison infographic: 'LWC Native State Management in Summer 26'. Deep navy blue bold header. LEFT COLUMN 'Wire Adapter Sprawl' soft red and grey with bold blue-grey header box: blocks for '@wire(getRecord) in 7 components', 'duplicate UI API calls', 'stale sibling component state' each with small warning icon. Central flow diagram showing tangled red lines between AccountHeader, OpportunityPanel, CaseTimeline, RenewalSummary. RIGHT COLUMN 'Native LWC Store' soft green and blue with bright blue header box: blocks for 'single accountWorkspaceStore', 'imperative load once', 'selectors for derived state'. Central glowing node 'Native State Store' with clean straight lines to Header, Panels, Actions. Speedometer labeled FAST pointing green. Clean vector SaaS aesthetic, deep navy, teal, and blue palette on light blue-grey background.
images:
A highly detailed, technical blueprint-style developer infographic poster on the theme of "WIRE ADAPTER vs NATIVE LWC STORE".
Overall Theme: High-tech dark mode developer dashboard, cybersecurity blueprint, with sharp glowing lines and neat borders.
Colors: Deep dark navy background with glowing neon blue, electric cyan, warning red, and success green highlights.

Header:
- A prominent top-center glowing blue header: "WIRE ADAPTER vs NATIVE LWC STORE".
- Below it, a clean subtitle: "Summer 26 State Management — What Changes in Your Component Code" with a small Salesforce logo on the top-left and an "APEX BEST PRACTICES" badge on the top-right.

Section 1: "WHY IT MATTERS" (Top Row, 3 Horizontal Cards)
- "9 → 3 API calls" with a chart icon, "One Spinner" with a clock icon, "Shared Freshness" with a layers icon

Section 2: "THE COST" (Middle Row, Side-by-Side Comparison)
- Left Box (Red Theme): "BEFORE: @wire Sprawl Across 7 Components". Shows a red status pill "DANGER — ANTI-PATTERN".
- Right Box (Green Theme): "AFTER: One Native Store, One Load, Shared State". Shows a green status pill "SUCCESS — BEST PRACTICE".

Section 3: "CODE PATTERNS" (Main Center)
- Left Column (Bad Code in Red Border):
  - Heading: "// BAD"
  - Code block:
    // 7 components each owning their own wire
@wire(getRecord, { recordId: '$recordId' })
wiredAccount  // in AccountHeader.js
@wire(getRecord, { recordId: '$recordId' })
wiredAccount  // in RiskBanner.js (duplicate!)
@wire(getOpenCases, { accountId: '$accountId' })
wiredCases    // in CasesPanel.js
// Result: 9 UI API calls on load, 4 spinners
- Right Column (Good Code in Green Border):
  - Heading: "// GOOD"
  - Code block:
    // Summer 26: one store, imperative load once
import { accountWorkspaceStore } from './store'
const { account, cases, signals } =
  accountWorkspaceStore.getState(recordId)
// ALL components subscribe to same state object
// One load, one refresh boundary, zero duplicates

Section 4: "DEVELOPER PROFILE & CHECKLIST" (Bottom Row)
- Left Side: A 3D Pixar-style cartoon avatar portrait of a dark-skinned South Asian male with a full thick black beard, shoulder-length wavy dark hair pulled up into a messy top bun, warm friendly smile showing teeth, wearing a grey crew-neck sweatshirt with the word "MAGNETIC" on it, set against a warm orange bokeh background. The avatar is labeled "Bennie Joseph | Salesforce Architect". Next to it is a quote bubble: "Architecting intuitive systems, engineering scalable solutions."
- Right Side: A checklist with points: "Create one store per workspace — not per component", "Load imperatively once in root component on connectedCallback", "Expose selectors for derived state — keep logic out of templates", "Use actions for updates — components never mutate store directly", "Add refresh boundary: store.invalidate() after Apex DML succeeds".
- Footer: A thin bar with icons for "LWC Summer 26", "Native State", "Wire Adapters", "UI API v64.0" and the text "CODE SMART. AUTOMATE FASTER. DELIVER EXCELLENCE."

Style Details: Super high resolution, technical drawing grid, razor-sharp white and neon text, crisp layout, developer IDE aesthetic, flat vector graphics with subtle glow, highly legible monospace font for code blocks, professional presentation.
